channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-11085] Replace Lustre interval tree with kernel one
item.description: Lustre created a internal interval tree based on the beta patch for the linux kernel. Its been long since merged into the kernel tree so Lustre can migrate to using that implementation
item.summary: Replace Lustre interval tree with kernel one

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Thanks Neil for abandoning the patch and congrats to all involved for completing this body of work!
what about https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53932? Should that be abandoned?
all patches landed.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54565/
Subject: LU-11085 tests: add a test case for same range lock
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 0e2f9f8568675a3f473767a9881aa1bc470ecc95
One patch left

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/49166/
Subject: LU-11085 lustre: remove interval-tree code
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: df0663f5a3a0b4ecb55ee67c07d1ced41620832b

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54587/
Subject: LU-11085 ldlm: optimise extent locks with identical extent
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 0cf356c4e4277d20324463b0cfcd4f414b7c5df9

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/41792/
Subject: LU-11085 ldlm: convert ldlm extent locks to linux extent-tree
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: d2ff746a997395e6a31d107e4026ae93c59aef87

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/34021/
Subject: LU-11085 ldlm: move interval_insert call from ldlm_lock to ldlm_extent
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: f5e73b3efb71b7d88d80389f84aef670a37210c3

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53931/
Subject: LU-11085 ldlm: save space in struct ldlm_lock
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: d199b178ed694dfafa3082604a440d08166067ce

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/33221/
Subject: LU-11085 ldlm: simplify use of interval-tree.
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: c5b8ac83bdc199e564df3a9c3636e9ad89c03ef3

If that is a credible pattern, then the current patch set won't handle it well.

I've uploaded a new patch (see above) which should handle the case better.

"Neil Brown <neilb@suse.de>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54587
Subject: LU-11085 ldlm: optimise extent locks with identical extent
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 2f6e26534df772094f8896b8c2739d6267539f5c

Hi, Neil, Such case is not so much on client side since lustre cache the locks for resource. But very usual on server side. I don't think this results are very precise. As it run on a VM box. Let us make a plan for real hardware testing. Then it maybe give us a solid base for decision.

Is the many-identical-extents pattern at all expected in practice?  I understand that having many whole-file extents is to be expected, but is there any reason to expect many identical extents that are not whole-file ?

"Yang Sheng <ys@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54565
Subject: LU-11085 tests: add a test case for same range lock
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: cd9c25e7cfa673875c9f6f61871f710a56de7625

Hi, Andreas, Neil,

As a preparation for real hardware testing. I have finished the comparison between Master & Neil's patch on my local VM box. I added a new case in ldlm_extent.ko to simulate for many same range locks work with interval tree. Since this is main difference between two cases.

LN=lock number, IN=interval entry number, it only make sense for master. Other case LN=IN

    master . . . Neil . . .
test loop min_iters iters ns/iter LN/IN min_iters iters ns/iter LN
0 0 100 589428 16967 10/10 100 593997 16836 10/10
0 1 1000 576186 17357 100/100 1000 583949 17126 100/100
0 2 10000 557462 17940 1000/1000 10000 563758 17739 1000/1000
0 3 100000 547001 18283 8000/8000 100000 548249 18241 8000/8000
0 4 1000000 542147 18447 8000/8000 1000000 543857 18389 8000/8000
0 5 1000000 538325 18578 8000/8000 1000000 546304 18306 8000/8000
0 6 1000000 548647 18228 8000/8000 1000000 551369 18138 8000/8000
0 7 1000000 541799 18458 8000/8000 1000000 525843 19018 8000/8000
0 8 1000000 542082 18449 8000/8000 1000000 550712 18160 8000/8000
0 9 1000000 542585 18432 8000/8000 1000000 546706 18293 8000/8000
0 10 1000000 541636 18464 8000/8000 1000000 536754 18632 8000/8000
0 11 1000000 544757 18358 8000/8000 1000000 547558 18264 8000/8000
0 12 1000000 544886 18354 8000/8000 1000000 550481 18167 8000/8000
0 13 1000000 538370 18576 8000/8000 1000000 535394 18679 8000/8000
0 14 1000000 529991 18870 8000/8000 1000000 552635 18096 8000/8000
0 15 1000000 540772 18493 8000/8000 1000000 549103 18213 8000/8000
0 16 1000000 518721 19280 8000/8000 1000000 528705 18916 8000/8000
0 17 1000000 535805 18665 8000/8000 1000000 549448 18201 8000/8000
0 18 1000000 521497 19177 8000/8000 1000000 578229 17295 8000/8000
0 19 1000000 531136 18829 8000/8000 1000000 513656 19470 8000/8000
0 20 1000000 532736 18772 8000/8000 1000000 534272 18718 8000/8000
0 21 1000000 554993 18020 8000/8000 1000000 540579 18500 8000/8000
0 22 1000000 537819 18595 8000/8000 1000000 545375 18338 8000/8000
0 23 1000000 540678 18497 8000/8000 1000000 548161 18244 8000/8000
0 24 1000000 537992 18589 8000/8000 1000000 550178 18177 8000/8000
0 25 1000000 553059 18083 8000/8000 1000000 553373 18072 8000/8000
0 26 1000000 536573 18638 8000/8000 1000000 561840 17800 8000/8000
0 27 1000000 535148 18688 8000/8000 1000000 555033 18018 8000/8000
0 28 1000000 532173 18792 8000/8000 1000000 556065 17985 8000/8000
0 29 1000000 529113 18901 8000/8000 1000000 547856 18254 8000/8000

This is test 1 result:

    master . . . Neil . . .
test loop min_iters iters ns/iter LN/IN min_iters iters ns/iter LN
1 0 100 300462 33285 10/6 100 303913 32907 10
1 1 1000 296773 33699 100/51 1000 294299 33982 100
1 2 10000 280353 35672 1000/501 10000 296380 33743 1000
1 3 100000 279950 35724 10000/5001 100000 289451 34551 10000
1 4 1000000 304053 32892 100000/50001 100000 295926 33795 100000
1 5 1000000 387661 25798 363431/181716 100000 298195 33538 100000
1 6 1000000 383801 26058 359812/179902 100000 293586 34065 100000
1 7 1000000 378832 26399 355154/177577 100000 298693 33482 100000
1 8 1000000 379368 26362 355656/177826 1000000 302796 33028 100000
1 9 1000000 386796 25856 362620/181309 1000000 364706 27422 341910
1 10 1000000 376779 26543 353229/176612 323343 323343 30930 303133
1 11 1000000 384872 25985 360816/180400 323343 344465 29033 322935
1 12 1000000 383039 26109 359098/179545 323343 335568 29803 314594
1 13 1000000 383018 26111 359078/179536 323343 364386 27446 323343
1 14 1000000 388018 25774 363765/181879 323343 362871 27560 323343
1 15 1000000 391751 25529 367265/183630 323343 360821 27717 323343
1 16 1000000 381317 26227 357483/178740 323343 359171 27844 323343
1 17 1000000 372426 26853 349148/174569 323343 362158 27615 323343
1 18 1000000 386006 25908 361879/180938 323343 355404 28140 323343
1 19 1000000 349886 28583 328017/164006 323343 361682 27651 323343
1 20 1000000 344231 29053 322715/161357 323343 359499 27819 323343
1 21 1000000 349089 28648 327270/163633 323343 362800 27566 323343
1 22 1000000 388696 25729 364401/182196 323343 365559 27358 323343
1 23 1000000 386776 25857 362601/181293 323343 364559 27433 323343
1 24 1000000 374628 26695 351212/175606 323343 367861 27187 323343
1 25 1000000 401900 24884 376780/188390 323343 348386 28706 323343
1 26 333007 333007 30032 312193/156097 323343 377811 26470 323343
1 27 333007 376307 26576 333007/166500 323343 383960 26046 323343
1 28 333007 367995 27177 333007/166501 323343 380211 26303 323343
1 29 333007 371337 26932 333007/166502 323343 377863 26467 323343

This is test2 result:
 

    master . . . Neil . . .
test loop min_iters iters ns/iter LN/IN min_iters iters ns/iter LN
2 0 100 573003 17453 10/1 100 577899 17305 10/10
2 1 1000 579613 17254 100/1 1000 448149 22316 100/100
2 2 10000 565089 17698 1000/1 10000 135065 74046 1000/1000
2 3 100000 583336 17144 10000/1 10000 20307 492493 10000/10000
2 4 1000000 578232 17295 20000/1 10000 19835 504220 10000/10000
2 5 1000000 590431 16938 20000/1 10000 19953 501246 10000/10000
2 6 1000000 588650 16990 20000/1 10000 20410 490023 10000/10000
2 7 1000000 595922 16782 20000/1 10000 20556 486552 10000/10000
2 8 1000000 591165 16917 20000/1 10000 18425 542819 10000/10000
2 9 1000000 579124 17269 20000/1 10000 20563 486381 10000/10000
2 10 1000000 583477 17140 20000/1 10000 21364 468130 10000/10000
2 11 1000000 574198 17417 20000/1 10000 19761 506130 10000/10000
2 12 1000000 583792 17131 20000/1 10000 21254 470553 10000/10000
2 13 1000000 571458 17500 20000/1 10000 20294 492858 10000/10000
2 14 1000000 585015 17095 20000/1 10000 20252 493852 10000/10000
2 15 1000000 590252 16943 20000/1 10000 20465 488710 10000/10000
2 16 1000000 595337 16798 20000/1 10000 13594 735792 10000/10000
2 17 1000000 561359 17815 20000/1 10000 16208 617076 10000/10000
2 18 1000000 571473 17500 20000/1 10000 16563 603868 10000/10000
2 19 1000000 588794 16985 20000/1 10000 17326 577236 10000/10000
2 20 1000000 575597 17375 20000/1 10000 16760 596765 10000/10000
2 21 1000000 576836 17337 20000/1 10000 16107 620957 10000/10000
2 22 1000000 588237 17001 20000/1 10000 17124 584095 10000/10000
2 23 1000000 581008 17213 20000/1 10000 16433 608606 10000/10000
2 24 1000000 589261 16972 20000/1 10000 16242 615799 10000/10000
2 25 1000000 577767 17309 20000/1 10000 16267 614865 10000/10000
2 26 1000000 582436 17170 20000/1 10000 16723 598083 10000/10000
2 27 1000000 572155 17479 20000/1 10000 16101 621148 10000/10000
2 28 1000000 578331 17292 20000/1 10000 16393 610084 10000/10000
2 29 1000000 584464 17111 20000/1 10000 17846 560459 10000/10000

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54204/
Subject: LU-11085 tests: Add performance test for ldlm_extent code
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 887889b333aa3499277959046153d347f93bac4a
Tested on latest master and it is real hardware (DDN 14K).

Here are the interval performance results for my testbed:

What Lustre version is being tested?

dlm_extent: start test 0
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=0 min_iters=100 iters=132536 ns/iter=75459
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=1 min_iters=1000 iters=19526 ns/iter=512209
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=2 min_iters=10000 iters=8005 ns/iter=1249487
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=3 min_iters=10000 iters=8038 ns/iter=1244347 
:

Is this real hardware or a VM? I was seeing min_iters=100000 on the Janitor testing. It might also be interesting to start with min_iters=10 to get more of an idea what the slope/curve is?

ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=0 min_iters=100 iters=65755 ns/iter=152096
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=1 min_iters=1000 iters=9648 ns/iter=1036624
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=2 min_iters=10000 iters=4007 ns/iter=2496235
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=3 min_iters=10000 iters=4019 ns/iter=2488494 
:

This is actually doing two lock enqueues per iteration, so we might consider to report 1/2 that value as "ns/lock" instead of (or in addition to) "ns/iter" to make these results more easily comparable? It appears to be the case that ns/lock is about the same for both cases as they increase min_iters, so at least this shows the "full-extent lock" does not add significant overhead as the number of locks increases.

Here are the interval performance results for my testbed:

ldlm_extent: sizeof(struct ldlm_lock)=544
ldlm_extent: start test 0
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=0 min_iters=100 iters=132536 ns/iter=75459
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=1 min_iters=1000 iters=19526 ns/iter=512209
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=2 min_iters=10000 iters=8005 ns/iter=1249487
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=3 min_iters=10000 iters=8038 ns/iter=1244347
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=4 min_iters=10000 iters=8040 ns/iter=1244059
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=5 min_iters=10000 iters=8062 ns/iter=1240602
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=6 min_iters=10000 iters=8060 ns/iter=1240833
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=7 min_iters=10000 iters=8031 ns/iter=1245334
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=8 min_iters=10000 iters=8020 ns/iter=1247099
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=9 min_iters=10000 iters=8045 ns/iter=1243194
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=10 min_iters=10000 iters=8031 ns/iter=1245432
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=11 min_iters=10000 iters=8016 ns/iter=1247658
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=12 min_iters=10000 iters=8074 ns/iter=1238779
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=13 min_iters=10000 iters=8052 ns/iter=1242090
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=14 min_iters=10000 iters=8048 ns/iter=1242697
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=15 min_iters=10000 iters=8040 ns/iter=1244002
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=16 min_iters=10000 iters=8075 ns/iter=1238650
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=17 min_iters=10000 iters=8045 ns/iter=1243145
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=18 min_iters=10000 iters=8042 ns/iter=1243724
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=19 min_iters=10000 iters=8029 ns/iter=1245760
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=20 min_iters=10000 iters=8036 ns/iter=1244569
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=21 min_iters=10000 iters=8047 ns/iter=1242942
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=22 min_iters=10000 iters=8037 ns/iter=1244513
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=23 min_iters=10000 iters=8044 ns/iter=1243436
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=24 min_iters=10000 iters=8037 ns/iter=1244403
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=25 min_iters=10000 iters=8033 ns/iter=1245130
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=26 min_iters=10000 iters=8031 ns/iter=1245376
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=27 min_iters=10000 iters=8053 ns/iter=1242005
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=28 min_iters=10000 iters=8024 ns/iter=1246414
ldlm_extent: test 0 loop=29 min_iters=10000 iters=8047 ns/iter=1242973
ldlm_extent: test 0 ended - loops=30 min_iters=10000 mean=1180544 stddev=243609
ldlm_extent: start test 1
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=0 min_iters=100 iters=65755 ns/iter=152096
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=1 min_iters=1000 iters=9648 ns/iter=1036624
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=2 min_iters=10000 iters=4007 ns/iter=2496235
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=3 min_iters=10000 iters=4019 ns/iter=2488494
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=4 min_iters=10000 iters=4024 ns/iter=2485968
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=5 min_iters=10000 iters=4016 ns/iter=2490637
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=6 min_iters=10000 iters=4022 ns/iter=2486690
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=7 min_iters=10000 iters=4020 ns/iter=2488403
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=8 min_iters=10000 iters=4011 ns/iter=2493755
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=9 min_iters=10000 iters=4023 ns/iter=2486562
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=10 min_iters=10000 iters=4019 ns/iter=2489039
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=11 min_iters=10000 iters=4018 ns/iter=2489499
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=12 min_iters=10000 iters=4022 ns/iter=2486879
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=13 min_iters=10000 iters=4032 ns/iter=2481017
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=14 min_iters=10000 iters=4031 ns/iter=2481085
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=15 min_iters=10000 iters=4022 ns/iter=2486930
[ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=16 min_iters=10000 iters=4026 ns/iter=2484634
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=17 min_iters=10000 iters=4025 ns/iter=2485359
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=18 min_iters=10000 iters=4021 ns/iter=2487570
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=19 min_iters=10000 iters=4029 ns/iter=2482615
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=20 min_iters=10000 iters=4016 ns/iter=2490459
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=21 min_iters=10000 iters=4020 ns/iter=2488434
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=22 min_iters=10000 iters=4028 ns/iter=2483339
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=23 min_iters=10000 iters=4019 ns/iter=2488912
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=24 min_iters=10000 iters=4025 ns/iter=2484793
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=25 min_iters=10000 iters=4020 ns/iter=2488146
ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=26 min_iters=10000 iters=4023 ns/iter=2486081

ldlm_extent: test 1 loop=28 min_iters=10000 iters=4017 ns/iter=2490122

ldlm_extent: test 1 ended - loops=30 min_iters=10000 mean=2361091 stddev=485801

Branch: master
Current Patch Set:

Oleg Drokin (green@whamcloud.com) merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/33854/
Subject: LU-11085 ldlm: change lock_matches() to return bool.
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: ec138c5c58dd74700b29ad960be4ac5a881a35b0

Attached is a tarball bugzilla-11300.tgz with the bugzilla ticket b=11300 comments and discussion (bugzilla.lustre.org is no longer available, but I made an archive some years ago that had all of the public tickets and attachments). Also attached is the High Level Design document IntervalTree_HLD.tgz that was written for this feature. It is in LyX format (LaTeX GUI editor), which I no longer have installed, but could be read as text, or installed and converted to PDF or similar.

Also of interest is likely patch https://review.whamcloud.com/28406 "LU-6245 tests: remove it_test" (v2_10_51_0-26-gebd0885c54) which deleted the unit test for the interval tree code. It looks like it had a built-in performance test, which could run in userspace.

James Simmons (uja.ornl@yahoo.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/33221

Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 849f7bbf36b49d67a71c7e54e222b4f1a95d83ce
Strange. Your account did not have the permissions to have tickets assigned. I know that you have had this in the past. I wonder why this had changed? Anyway, fixed now.
I can't assign this ticket to myself.
